tickets.title: [UX/UI] RevisiÃ³n integral de backlog + propuesta de simplificaciÃ³n
tickets.description: Revisar todos los tickets pendientes del proyecto personal-provider con foco en UX/UI y valor real para el MVP. Entregables: (1) listado de mejoras UX/UI accionables, (2) lista de tickets que pueden obviarse/postergarse sin afectar operaciÃ³n, (3) propuesta de consolidaciÃ³n/fusiÃ³n de tickets redundantes, (4) recomendaciÃ³n final de top prioridades para siguiente sprint. Crear un ticket resumen con hallazgos y justificaciÃ³n por item (impacto vs esfuerzo).

comments.text: Reasignado a carbon por directiva del usuario (owner de copy/QA y revisiÃ³n de backlog).

Hykar (fallback manual) - 5 mejoras de impacto inmediato (estabilidad/costo):
1) Probe de salud por cuenta basado en inferencia real OpenClaw (no /v1/models) para evitar falsos 403 por scopes OAuth login. Impacto: alto / Esfuerzo: medio.
2) PolÃ­tica de selecciÃ³n determinÃ­stica y visible: prioridad + desempate estable (updated_at/assignment_id) y ediciÃ³n rÃ¡pida desde UI. Impacto: alto / Esfuerzo: bajo.
3) Cooldown inteligente por cuenta exhausted/degraded con recuperaciÃ³n automÃ¡tica + jitter para evitar thundering herd al reset. Impacto: alto / Esfuerzo: medio.
4) Alertas accionables por agente (consecutivos fail, token <24h, failover repetido) y estado semÃ¡foro en /ui/accounts. Impacto: alto / Esfuerzo: bajo.
5) LÃ­mite de concurrencia por cuenta y budget guardrails por agente (hard/soft) para cuentas free, con desvÃ­o automÃ¡tico a fallback. Impacto: alto / Esfuerzo: medio.
RecomendaciÃ³n: ejecutar 1+2 primero, luego 3, y cerrar con 4+5.

Carbon (fallback de coordinaciÃ³n) - Entregable UX/UI para #198

1) 8 mejoras UX/UI prioritarias (impacto/esfuerzo):
- M1. Landing con navegaciÃ³n clara + CTA por flujo (Wizard / Cuentas / Monitor). Impacto: Alto / Esfuerzo: Bajo.
- M2. Tabla de cuentas con filtros por agente/estado/runtime y orden por prioridad. Impacto: Alto / Esfuerzo: Bajo.
- M3. Etiquetas visuales consistentes (active/degraded/exhausted/revoked) con semÃ¡foro. Impacto: Alto / Esfuerzo: Bajo.
- M4. Acciones rÃ¡pidas por fila (set primary, set fallback, pause/drain) con confirmaciÃ³n. Impacto: Alto / Esfuerzo: Medio.
- M5. Vista de asignaciones por agente con drag&drop de prioridad y guardado explÃ­cito. Impacto: Alto / Esfuerzo: Medio.
- M6. Timeline de eventos/auditorÃ­a con filtros por cuenta/agente/tipo. Impacto: Medio-Alto / Esfuerzo: Medio.
- M7. Mensajes de error accionables (quÃ© pasÃ³ + cÃ³mo arreglar) en wizard y monitor. Impacto: Medio-Alto / Esfuerzo: Bajo.
- M8. Checklist de salud visible (token expiry, refresh fail, failover count) por cuenta. Impacto: Alto / Esfuerzo: Medio.

2) Tickets que pueden obviarse/postergarse sin afectar MVP:
- #90 export CSV/JSON
- #106 archivado histÃ³rico de auditorÃ­a
- #108 SLA interno formal
- #117 aprobaciÃ³n de cambios
- #118 etiquetas por cuenta
- #119 notas por incidente
- #120 vista semanal

3) ConsolidaciÃ³n de tickets redundantes (propuesta):
- Grupo A (UI Operativa): #86 + #87 + #88 + #89 -> â€œConsola operativa unificadaâ€
- Grupo B (Observabilidad): #75 + #76 + #77 + #78 -> â€œObservabilidad base + dashboardsâ€
- Grupo C (Pruebas): #91 + #92 + #93 + #94 + #95 + #96 -> â€œPaquete QA tÃ©cnico MVPâ€
- Grupo D (Resiliencia OAuth/Refresh): #61 + #62 + #63 + #79 + #80 -> â€œResiliencia de credencialesâ€

Top 5 recomendado prÃ³ximo sprint:
1) #86 UI tabla filtrable
2) #87 vista por agente
3) #75 logging estructurado
4) #61 scheduler con jitter
5) #63 circuit breaker por cuenta

resolution.text: Propuesta integral de UX/UI y simplificaciÃ³n de backlog entregada. Se identificaron 8 mejoras prioritarias, se propuso la postergaciÃ³n de 7 tickets no crÃ­ticos para el MVP y se consolidaron los pendientes en 4 grupos operativos. El plan de ejecuciÃ³n inmediato se centra en la consola unificada y resiliencia de credenciales.

tickets.title: [CLW-A01] Claude solution - Classify openai-codex quota 429 (insufficient_quota / "You exceeded your current quota") as failover-eligible
tickets.description: Classify openai-codex quota 429 (insufficient_quota / "You exceeded your current quota") as failover-eligible.

comments.text: HEARTBEAT START: owner=hykar. Motivo: scope de estabilidad/rate-limit classifier. Siguiente paso verificable: mapear insufficient_quota + "You exceeded your current quota" a failover-eligible=true y subir diff + test unitario.

MILESTONE: took over stale ticket after inactivity alert. Implemented quota classifier for openai-codex errors: marks failover eligible (exhausted path) for HTTP 429, error code insufficient_quota, and message "You exceeded your current quota".

resolution.text: DONE: CLW-A01 implemented in src/server.ts (probeOpenAICodexQuota classifier). Failover-eligible exhausted path now triggers for status 429, insufficient_quota code, and "You exceeded your current quota" body message. Added regression test in tests/server.runtimeRouting.test.ts (runtime probe returns exhausted + errorCode insufficient_quota). Validated with vitest + build.

tickets.title: [CLW-A02] Claude solution - Implement auth-profile rotation for openai-codex on quota exhaustion before model fallback chain
tickets.description: Implement auth-profile rotation for openai-codex on quota exhaustion before model fallback chain.

comments.text: HANDOFF DEPENDENCIA: queda en cola para juan-cruz tras #199. Motivo: requiere señal failover-eligible de A01. Siguiente paso verificable: implementar rotación auth.order por profile con cooldown provider:profile-id antes de model.fallbacks.

START: taking #200 after #199 completion. Handoff reason: #199 stale in previous owner and dependency is now resolved by implemented classifier. Next verifiable step: implement profile rotation over openai-codex accounts before model fallback, with per-profile cooldown markers.

HEARTBEAT PROGRESS: ticket kept active after inactivity warning. Working line: implement profile-level rotation before model fallback using runtime state + assignment priority, with per-profile cooldown/exhausted markers from runtime events.
MILESTONE: runtime routing now prefers openai-codex profile rotation before cross-provider fallback when provider is not explicitly requested. This enforces codex-profile failover order for MVP.

resolution.text: DONE: CLW-A02 implemented for MVP. Active-account runtime routing rotates across openai-codex profiles first (profile-level failover) and only falls back to other providers/models when all codex profiles are exhausted. Uses per-account runtime exhaustion markers as cooldown boundary. Validated with new tests: (1) prefer codex secondary over other provider, (2) fallback to other provider only when all codex profiles exhausted. Build and tests passed.

tickets.title: [CLW-A03] Claude solution - Fix cooldown scope to provider:profile-id (avoid full-provider cooldown on per-profile 429)
tickets.description: Fix cooldown scope to provider:profile-id (avoid full-provider cooldown on per-profile 429).

comments.text: MILESTONE: profile-scoped cooldown behavior validated by routing tests: exhausting one openai-codex profile does not block other openai-codex profiles; selection continues with secondary codex profile. This confirms provider:profile scope behavior for MVP.

resolution.text: DONE (MVP scope): cooldown is effectively profile-scoped via account_runtime per account/profile. Exhausting one codex profile keeps other codex profiles eligible (verified in runtime routing tests). No provider-wide lockout applied for quota/rate-limit events.

tickets.title: [CLW-A04] Claude solution - Show fallback candidate chain in session start logs/messages
tickets.description: Show fallback candidate chain in session start logs/messages.

tickets.title: [CLW-B01] Claude solution - Extract email from OAuth JWT and use it in profile IDs (openai-codex:user@example
tickets.description: Extract email from OAuth JWT and use it in profile IDs (openai-codex:user@example.com).

comments.text: HEARTBEAT START: owner=juan-cruz. Motivo: implementación core OAuth/JWT. Siguiente paso verificable: extraer claim email del JWT en writeOAuthCredentials y generar profile_id openai-codex:<email> con fallback default-N.
MILESTONE: implemented JWT email extraction for openai-codex and auto profileId openai-codex:<email>.

resolution.text: DONE: CLW-B01 implemented. OAuth callback now extracts email from JWT claim https://api.openai.com/profile.email and uses profileId openai-codex:<email>. If email is unavailable and profileId is missing, it assigns default/default-2/default-3 automatically. Validated with new tests in tests/server.oauthPkce.test.ts and npm run build.

tickets.title: [CLW-B05] Claude solution - Fix OAuth refresh persistence: write new tokens atomically and verify post-write
tickets.description: Fix OAuth refresh persistence: write new tokens atomically and verify post-write.

comments.text: HEARTBEAT START: taking #207 as next MVP-critical stability item (OAuth refresh persistence). Reason: core runtime reliability; non-MVP UI items deferred. Next verifiable step: enforce atomic token write + read-back assertion on refresh path with regression test.
MILESTONE: token rotation now runs in BEGIN IMMEDIATE transaction, writes tokens, reads back persisted row, decrypts and verifies access/refresh/expires fields before commit.
CLOSE: added regression assertions in tests/accountsService.test.ts (rotated access+refresh persistence + expires update) and validated token refresh flow tests + build.

resolution.text: DONE: CLW-B05 implemented for MVP stability. rotateTokensById now performs atomic transaction (BEGIN IMMEDIATE), persists refreshed tokens, re-reads row, decrypts and verifies access_token/refresh_token and expires_at before COMMIT; on mismatch it ROLLBACKs and fails fast. Added regression checks in accountsService tests and validated tokenRefresh + build.

tickets.title: [CLW-C01] Claude solution - Build lightweight account verification probe for openai-codex OAuth accounts
tickets.description: Build lightweight account verification probe for openai-codex OAuth accounts.

comments.text: HEARTBEAT START: picked next MVP ticket #208 (account verification probe) after completing #207. Reason: core runtime observability without UI expansion. Next verifiable step: expose probe status fields (ok/limited/exhausted/error + quota/reset) and test endpoint behavior.

MILESTONE: /accounts/runtime/probe now persists verification snapshots to usage cache and returns account-level verification fields: quotaStatus (ok/limited/exhausted/error/expired), quotaRemainingPct, usedPercent, resetAt, httpStatus, lastVerifiedAt + statusSummary.

resolution.text: DONE (MVP): implemented lightweight account verification probe in runtime endpoint. Probe classifies account status (ok/limited/exhausted/error/expired), computes quota remaining %, persists checked snapshot in account_usage_cache, and exposes statusSummary. Added regression tests for insufficient_quota->exhausted and high usage->limited, plus build verification.

tickets.title: [CLW-C02] Claude solution - Schedule periodic verification for all registered accounts (default every 30m)
tickets.description: Schedule periodic verification for all registered accounts (default every 30m).

comments.text: HEARTBEAT START: taking #209 as next MVP step after #208. Reason: periodic verification is required for continuous account health without UI scope expansion. Next verifiable step: add scheduler (default 30m) to run runtime probe loop and publish summary event.

MILESTONE: added periodic account verification scheduler (ACCOUNT_VERIFY_INTERVAL_MINUTES, default 30m). Interval runs runtime probe for openai-codex accounts, emits per-profile structured logs (profileId/status/quotaRemainingPct/durationMs/timestamp), and publishes probe summary events.

resolution.text: DONE (MVP): periodic verification implemented. New scheduler runs every ACCOUNT_VERIFY_INTERVAL_MINUTES (default 30), executes runtime probe for openai-codex accounts, persists usage snapshots, logs profile status, and publishes accounts.runtime.probe.completed summary events. Added timer lifecycle cleanup on server close. Tests and build pass.

tickets.title: [CLW-C03] Claude solution - Expose authenticated API endpoint: GET /api/auth/profiles/status
tickets.description: Expose authenticated API endpoint: GET /api/auth/profiles/status.

comments.text: START: taking #210 as next MVP visibility API after #209 completion. Next verifiable step: expose authenticated /api/auth/profiles/status response using runtime+usage cache fields (status, quotaRemainingPct, error_last, last_verified_at, cooldown_until).

HEARTBEAT CHECKPOINT: #210 remains active for MVP API endpoint. Next verifiable step: implement authenticated GET /api/auth/profiles/status using runtime + usage cache fields (status, quota_remaining_pct, error_last, last_verified_at, cooldown_until).

HEARTBEAT CHECKPOINT: #210 remains active after stale warning. Next verifiable step unchanged: ship authenticated GET /api/auth/profiles/status with runtime+usage fields and token gate, then add endpoint test and close MVP gate.

MILESTONE: implemented authenticated GET /api/auth/profiles/status with gateway token validation (x-api-key or Authorization Bearer). Endpoint returns profile_id, email, provider, status, quota_remaining_pct, error_last, last_verified_at, cooldown_until using runtime + usage cache.

resolution.text: DONE (MVP): /api/auth/profiles/status endpoint delivered with API token auth gate. Supports x-api-key and Bearer token; returns profile status array without exposing raw tokens. Uses account_runtime + account_usage_cache to surface status, quota_remaining_pct, error_last, last_verified_at, cooldown_until. payload.text: HEARTBEAT START: picked next MVP ticket #208 (account verification probe) after completing #207. Reason: core runtime observability without UI expansion. Next verifiable step: expose probe status fields (ok/limited/exhausted/error + quota/reset) and test endpoint behavior.

MILESTONE: /accounts/runtime/probe now persists verification snapshots to usage cache and returns account-level verification fields: quotaStatus (ok/limited/exhausted/error/expired), quotaRemainingPct, usedPercent, resetAt, httpStatus, lastVerifiedAt + statusSummary.

payload.resolution: DONE (MVP): implemented lightweight account verification probe in runtime endpoint. Probe classifies account status (ok/limited/exhausted/error/expired), computes quota remaining %, persists checked snapshot in account_usage_cache, and exposes statusSummary. Added regression tests for insufficient_quota->exhausted and high usage->limited, plus build verification.

payload.text: HEARTBEAT START: taking #209 as next MVP step after #208. Reason: periodic verification is required for continuous account health without UI scope expansion. Next verifiable step: add scheduler (default 30m) to run runtime probe loop and publish summary event.

MILESTONE: added periodic account verification scheduler (ACCOUNT_VERIFY_INTERVAL_MINUTES, default 30m). Interval runs runtime probe for openai-codex accounts, emits per-profile structured logs (profileId/status/quotaRemainingPct/durationMs/timestamp), and publishes probe summary events.

payload.resolution: DONE (MVP): periodic verification implemented. New scheduler runs every ACCOUNT_VERIFY_INTERVAL_MINUTES (default 30), executes runtime probe for openai-codex accounts, persists usage snapshots, logs profile status, and publishes accounts.runtime.probe.completed summary events. Added timer lifecycle cleanup on server close. Tests and build pass.

payload.text: START: taking #210 as next MVP visibility API after #209 completion. Next verifiable step: expose authenticated /api/auth/profiles/status response using runtime+usage cache fields (status, quotaRemainingPct, error_last, last_verified_at, cooldown_until).

HEARTBEAT CHECKPOINT: #210 remains active for MVP API endpoint. Next verifiable step: implement authenticated GET /api/auth/profiles/status using runtime + usage cache fields (status, quota_remaining_pct, error_last, last_verified_at, cooldown_until).

HEARTBEAT CHECKPOINT: #210 remains active after stale warning. Next verifiable step unchanged: ship authenticated GET /api/auth/profiles/status with runtime+usage fields and token gate, then add endpoint test and close MVP gate.

MILESTONE: implemented authenticated GET /api/auth/profiles/status with gateway token validation (x-api-key or Authorization Bearer). Endpoint returns profile_id, email, provider, status, quota_remaining_pct, error_last, last_verified_at, cooldown_until using runtime + usage cache.

payload.resolution: DONE (MVP): /api/auth/profiles/status endpoint delivered with API token auth gate. Supports x-api-key and Bearer token; returns profile status array without exposing raw tokens. Uses account_runtime + account_usage_cache to surface status, quota_remaining_pct, error_last, last_verified_at, cooldown_until. Added endpoint tests for unauthorized, configured-token success, and missing-token 503; build and tests green.

payload.text: HEARTBEAT START (post-MVP): reactivating #211 to maintain throughput after MVP core closure. Motive: next highest-impact deliverable is UI visibility over completed status API. Next verifiable step: wire table to /api/auth/profiles/status with badges (ok/limited/exhausted/error/expired) and show last_verified_at + cooldown_until.

HEARTBEAT CHECKPOINT: observed usage refresh timeout event from UI/API polling. Action: keep #211 active and prioritize UX clarification around cache-vs-live source indicator to avoid false certainty. Next verifiable step: add visible source/check timestamp and optional force-live refresh flow in accounts UI.

MILESTONE: dashboard now shows explicit "OAuth actual" identity with resolved email/profile for active account, plus usage source/check timestamp (cache vs live context). This addresses operator visibility request about which email is currently authenticated.
